Summarize this article:

HEADLINE: Marchers protesting planned deportations block major freeway in Los Angeles 1 of 18 | Thousands of people protesting mas
SOURCE: Associated Press

ARTICLE:

Marchers protesting planned deportations block major freeway in Los Angeles Thousands of people protesting mass deportations planned by President Donald Trump marched in Southern California on Sunday. Demonstrators in downtown Los Angeles blocked a major freeway. Protests were also held in Dallas and McAllen, Texas. CORRECTS NAME OF THE FREEWAY - Law enforcement personnel stage in front of a group of demonstrators who shut down the 101 freeway during a protest calling for immigration reform Sunday, Feb. 2, 2025, in Los Angeles.
```json
{
  "summary": "Thousands of demonstrators protesting mass deportations planned by President Donald Trump took to the streets in Southern California and Texas on Sunday, February 2, 2025. In Los Angeles, protesters blocked the 101 freeway in downtown, calling for immigration reform. Similar protests were held in Dallas and McAllen, Texas. Law enforcement personnel were present at the scene as demonstrators shut down major traffic routes.",
  "key_facts": [
    "Thousands of people protested mass deportations planned by President Donald Trump on Sunday, February 2, 2025",
    "Demonstrators in downtown Los Angeles blocked the 101 freeway during the protest",
    "The protests were calling for immigration reform",
    "Additional protests were held in Dallas and McAllen, Texas",
    "Law enforcement personnel staged at the protest sites where demonstrators shut down freeways"
  ],
  "sources_represented": [
    "Associated Press"
  ]
}
```

parsed_output.summary: Thousands of demonstrators protesting mass deportations planned by President Donald Trump took to the streets in Southern California and Texas on Sunday, February 2, 2025. In Los Angeles, protesters blocked the 101 freeway in downtown, calling for immigration reform. Similar protests were held in Dallas and McAllen, Texas. Law enforcement personnel were present at the scene as demonstrators shut down major traffic routes.
key_facts: Thousands of people protested mass deportations planned by President Donald Trump on Sunday, February 2, 2025
Demonstrators in downtown Los Angeles blocked the 101 freeway during the protest
The protests were calling for immigration reform
Additional protests were held in Dallas and McAllen, Texas
Law enforcement personnel staged at the protest sites where demonstrators shut down freeways
